div1.entryFree: Good-year (O. Edd. good-ier, good-yeere, good-yere, and good-year) supposed to be corrupted from goujère, i. e. the French disease: the --s shall devour them, Lr. V, 3, 24. Used as a slight curse: we must give folks leave to prate: what, the g.! Wiv. I, 4, 129. what the g.! why are you sad? Ado I, 3, 1.* what the g.! one must bear, H4B II, 4, 64. what the g.! do you think I would deny her? H4B II, 4, 64
